title: Fujitsu Sovereign AI · SoftBank Physical AI Alliance · Record ¥11.7T FX Intervention
audio_label: Fujitsu Sovereign AI · SoftBank Physical AI Alliance
summary: Fujitsu anchors Japan's enterprise AI market with dual Anthropic/OpenAI partnerships and a sovereign deployment stack; SoftBank's Physical AI consortium swells to 30 firms ahead of NEDO designation. The Finance Ministry disclosed a record ¥11.73 trillion FX intervention as USD/JPY retraces to 159 despite the largest monthly yen-defense spend in history.

sections.title: Fujitsu Stakes Dual Frontier: Sovereign AI via Anthropic and OpenAI
sections.body:
Fujitsu (富士通) announced on May 27 dual partnerships with Anthropic and OpenAI, building a multi-AI integration layer with its Kozuchi platform and Takane LLM. The Anthropic partnership enables on-premise Claude deployment within critical-infrastructure environments. Under a 'Customer Zero' strategy, 100,000 group employees use the stack internally before client rollout. Japan AI spending is forecast at 28% CAGR through 2028.

Fujitsu's dual-model alignment positions it as integration partner of record for Japan's Digital Agency (デジタル庁) procurement requiring domestically operated AI — a structural competitive moat.
sections.takeaway: Sovereign stack + Customer Zero creates a durable differentiator for regulated-enterprise and government procurement that pure API resellers cannot replicate.

sections.title: SoftBank Physical AI Alliance Swells to 30 Firms
sections.body:
The Japan AI Foundation Model Development consortium disclosed May 28 that ~30 firms are in due diligence, including Yaskawa Electric (安川電機), Fanuc (ファナック), Asahi Kasei, Daikin, and KDDI. The target: a 1-trillion-parameter model trained on factory-floor sensor data — weight, temperature, spatial coordinates. NEDO public-private designation in June is the critical gate; METI's ¥6.7T subsidy framework supports it.

For investors in Fanuc, Yaskawa, or Kawasaki Robotics, co-investment = a government-backed call option on the next-generation factory automation software layer.
sections.takeaway: NEDO June designation is the binary catalyst — converts 30 corporate bets into a subsidized national infrastructure program.

sections.title: Record ¥11.73T FX Intervention — Yen Retraces to 159
sections.body:
Japan's Finance Ministry disclosed May 29 that authorities spent a record ¥11.73T (~$74B) in FX intervention (為替介入) between April 28–May 27 — first since July 2024, surpassing the prior monthly record of ¥9.79T. USD/JPY temporarily hit ¥155 then retraced to 159.3. BOJ kept policy rate at 0.75%; three dissenters favor 1.00%. Governor Ueda cited Middle East inflation risks but gave no June meeting signal. Finance Minister Katayama issued fresh verbal warnings Friday.

Structural tension: fiscal authorities spending at record scale while monetary policy lags — June 15–16 BOJ meeting is the critical binary.
sections.takeaway: A BOJ hold with no signal at June 15–16 likely tests ¥162–165 and triggers a second record intervention round.

title: Japan AI Alliance Swells to 30 Firms, Rapidus Enters Customer Phase, BOJ Gauge at 2.8%
summary: Japan's physical AI consortium expands from 4 founding members to ~30 companies as materials and logistics giants join the training-data coalition, with NEDO public-private conversion expected this summer. Simultaneously, Rapidus formally opens its customer engagement phase with a general PDK release greenlit for FY2026, while the BOJ's new underlying inflation gauge prints 2.8%—140bp above the official headline—keeping the June 16–17 policy meeting a live event.

sections.title: Japan's Physical AI Coalition Swells to 30 Firms — Materials and Logistics Giants Reframe the Consortium's Industrial Logic
sections.body:
The 日本AIファウンデーションモデル開発 (Nihon AI Faundēshon Moderu Kaihatsu) consortium — Japan AI Foundation Model Development — has attracted investment interest from approximately 30 companies beyond its four founding members, with roughly ten expected to formally commit capital in June. The prospective new investors, reported by Nikkei on May 28, include 旭化成 (Asahi Kasei), the diversified chemicals and advanced-materials group; 安川電機 (Yaskawa Denki), a tier-one industrial-robot and servo-motor manufacturer; 富士通 (Fujitsu); major heavy-industry firms; and large transportation conglomerates.

The strategic logic shifts meaningfully when materials and logistics companies are in the room. The consortium's founders — ソフトバンク (SoftBank), 日本電気 (NEC), ソニーグループ (Sony Group), and 本田技研工業 (Honda) — are targeting フィジカルAI (butsuri AI, physical AI): foundation models designed to perceive environments through sensors and issue commands to robot arms, factory actuators, and autonomous vehicles. US and Chinese models have been trained overwhelmingly on text and image data. Japan's differentiation thesis rests on operational data accumulated on manufacturing floors across the supply chain: 素材 (sozai, materials) properties from Asahi Kasei, motion and torque data from Yaskawa's servo systems, logistics cycle times from transport groups.

The joint venture filed a NEDO application in April 2026; if approved, the venture converts into a formal public-private program around summer 2026 and becomes eligible for up to ¥1 trillion ($6.3B) in five-year support. The model roadmap targets one trillion parameters by 2027, multimodal capability by 2029, and physical-world sensor data integration in the 2030s. Masayoshi Son's team has publicly floated participation reaching 100 companies and organizations.
sections.takeaway: NEDO conversion this summer would transform a corporate JV into a sovereign physical AI infrastructure project — watch for the approval announcement as the key catalyst.

sections.title: Rapidus Enters Customer Phase: NEDO Approves FY2026 Plan Covering PDK General Release and Full Chiplet Pilot Operations
sections.body:
On April 11, 2026, ラピダス (Rapidus) announced that Japan's NEDO has approved its fiscal year 2026 plan and budget for two commissioned 2nm semiconductor programs: front-end wafer processing under the Japan-U.S. collaboration framework with IBM, and back-end chiplet packaging via the newly operational Rapidus Chiplet Solutions (RCS) facility at 北海道千歳市 (Hokkaido Chitose-shi). The approval locks in FY2026 funding and formalizes the milestones Rapidus must deliver this fiscal year to remain on the 2027 mass-production timeline.

The front-end mandate centers on releasing a generally available プロセス設計キット (purosesu sekkei kitto, PDK) to customers, a step beyond the pre-release PDK shared with early adopters in FY2025. Without a stable, production-grade PDK, fabless chip designers cannot commit the engineering resources required to tape out on a new process node — so this is effectively Rapidus's commercial activation event. In FY2025, the company verified Japan's first 2nm ゲートオールアラウンド (GAA, gate-all-around) transistor operation on 300mm wafers at its IIM-1 plant in Chitose. FY2026 pivots from proof-of-concept to yield ramp and process stability.

The back-end story may ultimately matter as much as the wafer line. RCS prototyped an organic insulating film RDL interposer on a 600mm square panel in FY2025 — an industry first by panel dimension — and will now enter full-scale pilot operations to verify 2.xD and 3D packaging manufacturing processes. チップレット (chippuretto, chiplet) packaging is the architecture of choice for advanced AI accelerators. CEO 小池淳義 (Koike Atsuyoshi) stated plainly: 'steadily progressing toward the start of mass production targeted for 2027.'
sections.takeaway: PDK general release to customers in FY2026 is the commercial activation milestone — it marks the transition from R&D proof-of-concept to an actual foundry business with customer design pipelines.

vocab.zh: ゲートオールアラウンド

sections.title: BOJ's New Underlying Inflation Gauge Hits 2.8% — A 140bp Divergence From Headline CPI Keeps June Meeting Genuinely Live
sections.body:
On May 26, the 日本銀行 (Nihon Ginkō, Bank of Japan) released its newest underlying inflation gauge showing Japan's コア物価 (koa bukka, core prices) — defined as CPI excluding institutional one-off factors such as education subsidies and energy-related government support programs — rose 2.8% year-over-year in April, up from 2.5% in March. This new index sits meaningfully above the BOJ's 2% price-stability target and accelerated on a month-over-month basis for the second consecutive reading. It stands in stark contrast to Japan's official core CPI for April, which printed at just 1.4% — the lowest reading since March 2022.

The 140 basis-point divergence between the two measures is the deliberate analytical purpose of the new gauge. The standard core CPI is artificially compressed by ongoing government subsidy programs. The BOJ's new underlying index strips these out to expose what inflation actually looks like absent fiscal intervention: a 2.8% reading means demand-pull and cost-push pressures are running at nearly double the headline. This creates a pointed dilemma heading into the June 16–17 金融政策決定会合 (Kin'yū Seisaku Kettei Kaigō, Monetary Policy Meeting): the official CPI is soft enough to justify holding the policy rate at 0.75%, but the underlying trend argues for resumed 利上げ (riagé, rate hike) normalization.

USD/JPY is trading near ¥159, a level that has previously drawn 財務省 (Zaimushō, Ministry of Finance) commentary on FX intervention. The OECD, in its May 13 economic surveys, projected the BOJ policy rate will reach 2% by end-2027, implying roughly 125 basis points of additional tightening. Deputy Governor 氷見野良三 (Himino Ryōzō) has reaffirmed commitment to further hikes; the question is timing. A hold is priced in for June, but the 2.8% underlying gauge narrows the bar for a hawkish surprise.
sections.takeaway: The 140bp gap between official CPI (1.4%) and the BOJ's new underlying gauge (2.8%) is a time bomb for investors anchored to the headline — subsidy phase-out will eventually close this gap, exposing the true inflation trajectory.

vocab.en: core prices; BOJ underlying inflation gauge

title: Japan's Physical AI Sovereign Bet, METI Boosts Rapidus with ¥631.5B, BOJ Holds at 0.75%
summary: Japan's largest industrial and financial groups deepen their sovereign physical AI consortium backed by ¥1 trillion in NEDO support, while METI approves a fresh ¥631.5B for Rapidus's active 2nm pilot line in Hokkaido, bringing cumulative government support to ¥2.35 trillion. The BOJ holds rates at 0.75% on a split vote but upgrades its CPI forecast to 2.8%, with the OECD projecting a 2% terminal rate by end-2027.

sections.title: Japan Launches Sovereign Physical AI Consortium — SoftBank, NEC, Sony, Honda Bet ¥1 Trillion on Domestic Foundation Model
sections.body:
On April 12, four of Japan's largest industrial groups — SoftBank (ソフトバンク), NEC (日本電気), Sony Group (ソニーグループ), and Honda (本田技研工業) — established a joint venture called Japan AI Foundation Model Development (日本AIファウンデーションモデル開発). Each founding member holds a stake exceeding 10%. Industrial heavyweights Nippon Steel and Kobe Steel, as well as the three megabanks — MUFG, SMBC, and Mizuho — have also taken equity positions.

The strategic logic is focused on physical AI (物理AI, butsuri AI): foundation models designed for real-world control tasks — perceiving environments through sensors and issuing commands to robot arms, factory actuators, and autonomous vehicles. The model targets roughly one trillion parameters and will be trained exclusively on Japanese operational data. SoftBank and NEC will lead model development; Honda will be the first external deployer; Sony's involvement covers robotics and gaming hardware. Preferred Networks (PFN) is participating in R&D.

Japan's NEDO has earmarked approximately ¥1 trillion ($6.3B) over five years beginning in FY2026. METI targets a 30% share of the global physical AI market by 2040 — backed by Japan's existing position controlling ~70% of global industrial robot manufacturing.
sections.takeaway: This is sovereign AI infrastructure backed by Japan's entire industrial establishment, with a built-in physical deployment network that US and EU rivals lack.

vocab.zh: プリファードネットワークス

vocab.en: New Energy & Industrial Technology Development Organization

sections.title: METI Approves Additional ¥631.5B for Rapidus — 2nm Pilot Line Active in Hokkaido, Mass Production Target Holds at FY2027
sections.body:
Japan's METI approved an additional ¥631.5 billion (~$4 billion) for state-backed chipmaker Rapidus (ラピダス), bringing total government R&D support to approximately ¥2.35 trillion since founding in August 2022. The fresh tranche targets IIM-1 in Chitose City, Hokkaido, where engineers are running test wafers through a two-nanometer gate-all-around (GAA) process developed with IBM. METI also announced NEDO will support semiconductor design projects alongside Fujitsu and IBM Japan to build domestic demand.

Working two-nanometer prototypes are being produced, and CEO Atsuyoshi Koike (小池淳義) has maintained the FY2027 mass-production timeline. TSMC and Samsung both entered 2nm volume production in 2025, meaning Rapidus will launch roughly two years behind; but its model is a high-mix, low-volume, fast-turnaround foundry service aimed at customers seeking alternatives to Taiwan concentration risk. Early confirmed clients include Fujitsu and Tenstorrent.

The equity structure now positions the Japanese government as Rapidus's largest single shareholder, holding 11.5% of voting rights plus a golden-share veto. For institutional investors, Rapidus is no longer a venture bet but a strategic infrastructure program.
sections.takeaway: Rapidus has crossed from paper project to functioning pilot line — 2027 mass-production timeline is live, but customer pipeline depth is the make-or-break variable.

vocab.zh: ゲートオールアラウンド

sections.title: BOJ Holds at 0.75% on Split Vote, Lifts CPI Forecast to 2.8% — OECD Projects Terminal Rate of 2% by End-2027
sections.body:
The Bank of Japan (日本銀行) left its policy rate (政策金利, seisaku kinri) unchanged at 0.75% at its April 27-28 meeting, but the decision was fractious: three of nine board members dissented, proposing an immediate increase to 1.0%. The BOJ's core CPI forecast was raised to 2.8% from its prior 1.9% projection — a substantial revision reflecting both imported energy cost pressure and underlying wage-price dynamics. Spring wage negotiations (春闘, shuntō) produced average raises exceeding 5% for the second consecutive year.

BOJ Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino (氷見野良三) signaled the board intends to continue raising the policy rate in accordance with economic and price developments. The OECD's May 13 economic outlook projects the BOJ benchmark reaching 2% by end-2027 — roughly five additional 25-basis-point increments. That trajectory would make JGBs materially more competitive with global fixed income and put further pressure on the yen carry trade.

For equity investors, the rate path has asymmetric read-throughs. Banks — MUFG, SMFG, and Mizuho — benefit from net interest margin expansion at each increment, adding approximately ¥100-150B in annual net interest income per 25bps across the three megabanks. Export-oriented manufacturers face yen appreciation and higher domestic borrowing cost headwinds.
sections.takeaway: The BOJ's 6-3 split vote and 2.8% CPI revision signal the tightening cycle is real — the June 16-17 meeting and May CPI print (due June 20) are the next pivotal data points.

title: NEC–Anthropic Japan Partnership, TSMC Kumamoto 3nm Upgrade, CPI Falls to 1.4%
summary: NEC becomes Anthropic's first Japan-based global partner, deploying Claude across 30,000 engineers in a landmark enterprise AI deal. TSMC locks in $17B to upgrade its Kumamoto plant to 3nm, cementing Kyushu as a high-end semiconductor cluster. Japan's April CPI falls to a four-year low of 1.4%, scrambling the BOJ's rate-hike calculus ahead of June.

sections.title: NEC Becomes Anthropic's First Japan-Based Global Partner, Deploying Claude to 30,000 Engineers
sections.body:
NEC Corporation announced it has become the first Japan-based company to achieve Global Partner status with Anthropic, gaining priority access to Claude models and joint go-to-market rights across enterprise clients. The initial deployment targets NEC's own 30,000-engineer workforce, making it one of the largest enterprise Claude rollouts announced in Asia.

The partnership gives NEC privileged positioning in Japan's fast-moving enterprise AI market, where domestic companies have historically deferred to US platforms for foundation models. NEC's move is significant because it simultaneously supports the Japan AI Foundation Model Development consortium — a direct rival to US AI platforms — while securing commercial access to Claude, illustrating the dual-track hedging strategy common among Japan's leading technology firms.
sections.takeaway: NEC is running both sides of Japan's AI bet — backing the sovereign domestic model JV while locking in Anthropic distribution rights — a hedge that reflects the genuine uncertainty about which stack will win enterprise workloads.

vocab.zh: グローバルパートナー

sections.title: TSMC Upgrades Kumamoto JASM-2 to 3nm, Locking In $17B and Kyushu as a High-End Node Cluster
sections.body:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company confirmed it will upgrade its second Kumamoto plant (JASM-2) to produce 3nm-class chips, a significant step up from the 6–12nm nodes planned at its first Kumamoto facility. The total investment commitment for both plants now reaches approximately $17 billion, with the Japanese government covering roughly half through subsidies under the Japan Semiconductor Industry Revitalization program.

The Kyushu semiconductor cluster is now anchored by TSMC at 3nm, Rapidus at 2nm (in Hokkaido), and a supporting ecosystem of materials suppliers including Shin-Etsu Chemical and SUMCO. The 3nm upgrade positions Kumamoto to serve automotive AI chip demand — Toyota, Honda, and Denso are all within the supply chain — without requiring the extreme EUV tooling density of TSMC's Taiwan fabs.
sections.takeaway: TSMC's 3nm commitment in Kumamoto bifurcates Japan's chip strategy into a near-term foreign-anchor approach (TSMC Kyushu) and a long-term sovereign play (Rapidus Hokkaido), with both receiving massive public subsidy.

vocab.en: Kumamoto — city in Kyushu, SW Japan
Kyushu — southernmost of Japan's four main islands

sections.title: Japan April CPI Falls to Four-Year Low at 1.4%, Scrambling BOJ's Rate Path Ahead of June Meeting
sections.body:
Japan's April consumer price index came in at 1.4% year-on-year, its lowest reading since 2022, as government energy subsidies and base effects suppressed headline figures. The print was well below the BOJ's 2.8% inflation forecast and below the 2% target, creating an acute tension with the BOJ's stated intention to continue raising rates.

The disinflation reading dramatically complicates the June 16-17 meeting calculus. Markets had priced approximately 35% odds of a hike to 1.0% following the April 28 meeting's hawkish dissent; post-April CPI, that probability has repriced sharply lower. BOJ watchers note the divergence between government-suppressed headline CPI and underlying services inflation, which remains above 2% — the metric BOJ Governor Ueda has indicated is the more relevant signal for policy normalization.
sections.takeaway: April CPI at 1.4% gives the BOJ political cover to hold in June, but the services inflation divergence means the underlying tightening bias hasn't changed — a hold in June likely extends the uncertainty into September.

vocab.en: consumer price index / CPI (shōhisha bukka shisū)

title: Japan AI Alliance, Rapidus 2nm Pilot Active, BOJ Holds at 0.75%
summary: Japan's four largest tech and industrial firms form a domestic foundation-model venture backed by NEDO's ¥1 trillion AI fund, while Rapidus confirms an active 2nm pilot line in Chitose. The Bank of Japan holds rates at 0.75% with a divided 6-3 vote, raising its inflation forecast sharply.

sections.title: Japan AI Alliance: SoftBank, Sony, NEC, Honda Form Domestic Foundation-Model Venture
sections.body:
Four of Japan's largest technology and industrial conglomerates established a joint venture to develop Japanese-language and physical-AI foundation models domestically. SoftBank, Sony, NEC, and Honda each hold more than 10%, with additional equity from Nippon Steel, Kobe Steel, and the three megabanks. Preferred Networks (PFN) is contributing engineers on a secondment basis.

The venture will apply for NEDO funding from a ¥1 trillion program running FY2026-FY2031. The target is high-performance models on domestic infrastructure and physical AI for factory automation and robotics.
sections.takeaway: The cap table spanning industrials, financials, and tech signals keiretsu-scale commitment; Fanuc and Yaskawa are well-positioned if the physical-AI mandate gains traction.

sections.title: Rapidus Lands ¥267.6B Round, Pilot Line Active in Chitose — 2nm Mass Production Target Holds at 2027
sections.body:
Rapidus closed a ¥267.6 billion financing round: ¥100B from IPA under METI, plus ¥167.6B from 32 private investors including Canon, Fujitsu, NTT, SoftBank, Sony Group, and Fujifilm. The Japanese government took an 11.5% voting stake plus a golden share granting veto power.

IIM-1, Rapidus's first plant in Chitose, Hokkaido, has transitioned from construction to an active pilot line running 2nm gate-all-around test wafers co-developed with IBM and imec. The company is in active discussions with 60+ potential customers. Mass production target remains 2027.
sections.takeaway: Rapidus's small-batch, fast-turnaround proposition targets a segment TSMC's gigafab model ignores; Fujifilm's co-development role is a commercial signal, not symbolic.

sections.title: BOJ Holds at 0.75%, Raises Inflation Forecast to 2.8% — OECD Sees 2% Rate by End-2027
sections.body:
The Bank of Japan held its short-term policy rate at 0.75% at its April 28 meeting, passing 6-3. Three dissenting members pushed for an immediate hike to 1.0%. The BOJ raised its FY2026 core inflation forecast to 2.8% from 1.9%, citing higher crude oil prices tied to the Iran conflict, while cutting FY2026 GDP to 0.5% from 1.0%.

The OECD projects the BOJ policy rate reaching 2.0% by end-2027, advising continued gradual hikes. Governor Ueda maintains a data-dependency posture, leaving the June 16-17 meeting open.
sections.takeaway: The 6-3 vote makes next-hike timing less predictable; export-oriented names with USD revenues provide natural yen hedges.
